entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The primary cereal crop discussed in the context of weed management in the Asian-Pacific region

entities.name: WEED MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of controlling unwanted plants in agricultural fields to ensure sustainable crop production

entities.name: ASIAN-PACIFIC REGION
entities.description: The geographical area encompassing countries in Asia and the Pacific where rice weed management is studied

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Environmental shifts that impact the effectiveness and nature of weed management

entities.name: HERBICIDE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The inherited ability of weeds to survive exposure to herbicides

entities.name: WEEDS
entities.description: Unwanted plants that compete with rice for nutrients, water, and light

entities.name: HERBICIDE TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of rice varieties to withstand herbicide application without damage

entities.name: BIOCONTROL
entities.description: The use of biological agents to manage weed populations

entities.name: ALLELOPATHY
entities.description: The chemical inhibition of one plant by another, used as a mechanism for weed management

entities.name: WEEDY RICE
entities.description: A weed species that closely resembles cultivated rice and competes with it

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: A sub-region of the Asian-Pacific region focusing on rice weed management
